reasoning: `leads_to_unfold` is external_body bridging `leads_to`'s definition — `always(p.implies(eventually(q)))` with nested `TempPred::new` closures — to a bare forall in the ensures. Unverified trust assumption resolving the intensional closure gap.

`implies_apply` is external_body bridging `p.implies(q).satisfied_by(ex)` — which evaluates a fresh `TempPred::new` closure — to the direct modus ponens conclusion. Separate unverified trust assumption.

`always_propagate_forwards` is external_body shifting `always(p)` to a suffix, requiring that `forall |j| p.satisfied_by(ex.suffix(i).suffix(j))` follows from `forall |j| p.satisfied_by(ex.suffix(j))` — bridges the suffix composition intensional gap without proof.

Moving a condition `c` from the leads_to antecedent into `always(c)` on the spec side is a correct and standard temporal logic transformation. The spec strengthening is the intended behavior, not a gap.
An unsatisfiable spec vacuously entailing anything is standard ex falso quodlibet. This is not a spec gap.
